question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the /network/stake endpoint to return HTTP 200 with a malformed or empty body, causing restNetworkStakeHealth() to return Status.UP and allowing the health() chain to proceed to subscribing() which may falsely confirm UP status despite incorrect record exports?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the static UNKNOWN Mono instance being shared across all reactive invocations, causing a scenario where a previous health() call's UNKNOWN result is reused in a subsequent call due to Mono caching, masking a change in record export status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the static UP Mono instance being shared and potentially cached, causing health() to return a stale UP status even after the mirror node has stopped exporting records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the static DOWN Mono instance being shared, causing a scenario where a DOWN result from one health check context bleeds into another concurrent health check, producing incorrect health status that misrepresents the record export state?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the flatMap(n -> UP) in subscribing() to be triggered by a very small but non-zero aggregated rate (e.g., 0.001 tx/s), causing the health indicator to report UP even though the effective record export rate to the mirror node is negligibly low and records are practically missing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter(sum -> sum <= 0) condition in publishing() by causing exactly zero net rate through transaction cancellations or errors, triggering getHealthForZeroRate() and returning UNKNOWN, which prevents detection of incorrect record exports?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 5-second timeout in restNetworkStakeHealth() by introducing exactly 5 seconds of latency on the /network/stake endpoint, causing a TimeoutException that sets Status.DOWN and prevents the health check from evaluating actual record export correctness?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: recordHealthMetric()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CLUSTER_UP AtomicInteger being a static field shared across all SubscriberHealthIndicator instances, so that in a multi-instance deployment, one instance's health result overwrites another's, causing the Prometheus gauge to reflect incorrect export health?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: registerGauge()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that registerGauge() registers the CLUSTER_UP gauge at @PostConstruct time with a fixed tag type=cluster, and if the MeterRegistry is shared, cause a duplicate metric registration exception that crashes the health indicator and prevents any record export monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive chain's error propagation—where an unhandled exception in publishing() or subscribing() propagates as an error signal—to cause health() to emit an error instead of a health status, leaving CLUSTER_UP unchanged at its last value and hiding missing record exports?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user cause mirrorSubscriber.getSubscriptions() to throw a runtime exception inside the reactive chain, causing subscribing() to propagate an error signal that bypasses getHealthForZeroRate() and leaves the health status as the last known value, hiding that records are no longer being exported?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user cause transactionGenerator.scenarios() to return an empty Flux, making the reduce() in publishing() emit 0.0, which passes the filter(sum -> sum <= 0) and calls getHealthForZeroRate(), returning UNKNOWN and masking the absence of record exports?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that restNetworkStakeHealth() logs the full error message including the exception message via log.error(statusMessage), and craft a network error with a message containing sensitive path information that gets logged and potentially exposed in log aggregation systems?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ordering of restNetworkStakeHealth() -> publishing() -> subscribing() in the health() chain, where a persistent UNKNOWN from restNetworkStakeHealth() short-circuits the entire chain, preventing any detection of missing or incorrect record exports at the subscription layer?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that subscribing() uses getRate() which is based on a 20-second rolling window (UPDATE_INTERVAL in AbstractScenario), so that a burst of activity followed by silence still reports a non-zero rate for up to 20 seconds, causing subscribing() to return UP while records are actually missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 20-second rolling window in Scenario.getRate() used by publishing() to create a false impression of active publishing, causing publishing() to emit empty (no health signal) and allowing subscribing() to return UP even when records have stopped being exported?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doOnNext(this::recordHealthMetric) side effect in health() to cause a race condition where the health metric is recorded before the Mono completes, leading to CLUSTER_UP reflecting an intermediate state that does not accurately represent the current record export health?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the WebClient used in getNetworkStakeStatusCode() by causing HTTP response splitting or header injection in the /network/stake response, causing the status code parsing to misclassify the response and return an incorrect health status that hides missing record exports?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that restNetworkStakeHealth() only checks the HTTP status code of /network/stake and not the response body content, so that a 200 OK response with an empty or stale network stake body causes the health check to return UP while the mirror node is actually not exporting records correctly?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that subscribing() does not validate the content or correctness of subscribed records—only their rate—to cause the health indicator to report UP while the mirror node is exporting incorrect or corrupted records at a normal rate?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the health() method has no rate limiting or debouncing, allowing them to hammer the health endpoint to cause excessive reactive pipeline instantiation, exhausting thread pool resources and causing legitimate health checks to time out, hiding the true record export status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restNetworkStakeHealth()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of retry logic in restNetworkStakeHealth() so that a single transient network error causes the health check to return DOWN or UNKNOWN, triggering false alerts about missing record exports when the mirror node is actually functioning correctly?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that health() creates a new reactive pipeline on every invocation with no caching, causing the health status to fluctuate rapidly between calls if the underlying state is borderline, making it impossible to reliably detect persistent missing record exports?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribing()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reduce(0.0, (cur, next) -> cur + next) in subscribing() to cause floating-point overflow or NaN if a subscription reports an extremely large rate, causing the filter(sum -> sum > 0) to behave unexpectedly and return an incorrect health status that masks missing record exports?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/SubscriberHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: publishing()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reduce(0.0, (c, n) -> c + n) in publishing() to cause floating-point underflow or NaN if a publish scenario reports a negative rate, causing the filter(sum -> sum <= 0) to incorrectly trigger and return DOWN/UNKNOWN, masking active record exports?
